This year I've jumped bracket (15% 25%), thanks having real (not graduate student stipend) working real hard idea back into lower bracket saving more retirement.
have $40,000 taxable income this year. bracket starts $34,000. figured that opened retirement account that uses pre-tax dollars (like individual 401K), could claim that deduction. contributed $6,001 that fund, deduct that have taxable income $33,999, putting bracket. Instead owing $40,000 (which $10,000), $33,999 (which $5,100). have locked $6,001 save $4,900.
asked some people about this scheme.
read some money blogs (investing retirement, frugality, etc.) decided them about scheme. wrote blog post answering question (and renamed me), didn't really address scheminess scheme. Sure, it's great save more retirement, doesn't make much sense have IRAs Roth Traditional), would really reduce burden dramatically?
didn't understand brackets work. thought that $33,999 taxable income (total=$5,100) $34,000 taxable income (total=$8,500), with middle ground. it's like that. EVERYBODY pays first $8,375, then next $25,625, then amount over that, forth.
carried scheme, burden $33,999) would $4,681. Without opening retirement account, have taxable income $40,000, burden $6,181. would really only save $1,500, which what you'd expect from $6,000 deduction bracket.
realize this jibberish. point that there isn't much gained lost) jumping brackets. It's that deal.
I'll probably open 401K anyway. doesn't hurt save money when possible.
